name: R2 -- Hermetic End-to-End Consumer-Bootstrap Test + CI Job

design_notes.bootstrap_sh_interface_findings: bootstrap.sh (read in full before authoring this contract) is ALREADY hermetic-capable, confirming spec Sec 4.2's no-runtime-addition claim: it accepts `--repo-url URL` (any URL string, including `file://<path>` -- there is no scheme allow-list, only a `require_pinned_tag` refusal of the TAG value, never the URL), `--tag TAG` (a pinned semver-shaped tag; `require_pinned_tag` refuses only the literal empty string, `main`, `master`, `HEAD`, and any `refs/heads/*` pattern -- an ephemeral tag name such as `e2e-<epoch>-<pid>` is NOT in that refusal set and is accepted), and `--target DIR` (overrides `NIZAM_TARGET_DIR`, default `.nizam`). Default mode (`run_install`) does exactly `git clone --depth 1 --branch "${GOVERNANCE_TAG}" "${GOVERNANCE_REPO_URL}" "${CLONE_DIR}"` -- a real, but fully local (no DNS/socket) `file://` transport clone when given a `file://` URL -- then copies `REQUIRED_MODULE_DIRS` (`standard`, `templates`, `schema`, `tools`) plus `NIZAM.json` into a staged temp dir, runs `check_required_files` + `check_nizam_index`, writes `provenance.json` (recording `tag`+`source_url`+`framework_version`+`installed_at`), and atomically moves the stage into `TARGET_DIR`. `--verify-only` mode (`run_verify_only`) is a separate, pure, network-free path: `check_required_files` + `check_nizam_index` + `check_provenance_tag` against an ALREADY-injected target, comparing `provenance.json`'s recorded tag to the `--tag` given at invocation -- no clone, no write. bootstrap.sh injects EXACTLY `standard/`, `templates/`, `schema/`, `tools/`, `NIZAM.json`, plus the `provenance.json` it writes itself -- it does NOT inject `methodology/`, `registry/`, `docs/`, `CONTEXT.md`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, or itself. `tools/skill.json` is a real, tracked file that ships wholesale inside the `tools/` module dir (confirmed: `tools/skill.json` exists at repo root today, is non-empty, and is copied by the `cp -r -- "${CLONE_DIR}/tools" "${STAGE_DIR}/tools"` step), so after injection the DOCUMENTED discovery path resolves at `<target>/tools/skill.json` -- this is the exact H4 regression surface (tools/interface.md Sec 2 item 1: 'Bootstrapped-consumer discovery' names `.nizam/tools/skill.json` as the first hop). NO CHANGE to bootstrap.sh is needed or made: no local-path mode, no dry-run flag, no additional CLI surface -- `--repo-url file://$(pwd)` + a synthetic `--tag` + `--target <scratch>` is sufficient and is exactly the fallback path spec Sec 4.2 pre-authorizes without requiring it.
design_notes.hermetic_mechanism: tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh is a NEW, standalone script, DUAL-MODE like tools/verify_lib.sh's sourceable-library discipline (F-023 precedent) so its own assertion functions can be fixture-tested in isolation without paying a full clone's cost each time: it defines functions only (zero side effects from `source`-ing it) and calls `main "$@"` ONLY when directly executed, guarded by the standard `[[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]` idiom -- sourcing it for contract/QA fixture-testing therefore never triggers a real clone. Direct execution (`bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh` or `... --self-test-negative`) runs `main`, which: (1) creates a uniquely-named ANNOTATED tag (`git tag -a "e2e-$(date +%s)-$$" -m '<message>' HEAD` -- annotated, not lightweight, per spec Sec 4.2's explicit requirement and because bootstrap.sh's `require_pinned_tag` only refuses specific floating-branch literals, never annotated-vs-lightweight, so either would technically pass bootstrap.sh, but the spec is explicit that it must be annotated) on the working checkout's own HEAD -- no new commit, no push, no remote; (2) creates a `mktemp -d` scratch dir and targets `<scratch>/.nizam` as the injection target; (3) invokes `bash bootstrap.sh --repo-url "file://$(pwd)" --tag "${tag}" --target "${target}"` -- a real `git clone --depth 1 --branch <tag> file://...` (local filesystem transport only, confirmed no DNS/socket activity: `file://` clones read the source's object store directly); (4) runs the three positive assertions (payload-present, index-valid, discovery-path) plus `bash bootstrap.sh --verify-only --tag "${tag}" --target "${target}"`; (5) `trap cleanup EXIT` unconditionally deletes the scratch dir (`rm -rf`) and the ephemeral tag (`git tag -d`), following `set -e`+`local exit_code=$?; ...; return "${exit_code}"` exactly as bootstrap.sh's own `cleanup()` does, so a failure mid-run still cleans up and the trap never masks the real exit code. `--self-test-negative` runs the identical create-tag/clone/inject cycle into its OWN separate scratch dir (never touching the positive-path's resources), then deliberately `rm -f "${target}/tools/skill.json"` before re-invoking the SAME `assert_discovery_path` function (no divergent negative-path implementation) against the now-broken target -- proving the guard genuinely fires on the SAME code path the happy path trusts, not a tautological separate stub.
design_notes.assertion_design: Four assertion functions, each independently sourceable/fixture-testable: (a) `assert_payload_present <target>` -- plain `test -d` (NEVER `vlib_path_resolves`, deliberately: `vlib_path_resolves` trivially returns 0 on any token ending in `/` as its documented directory-only exemption, which would make a directory-presence check VACUOUS -- a genuine soundness trap this contract explicitly avoids) for each of `standard`, `templates`, `schema`, `tools`; plain `test -s` for `NIZAM.json` and `provenance.json` (bootstrap.sh writes both). (b) `assert_nizam_index_valid <target>` -- an INDEPENDENT python3 snippet (stdlib `json` only, no import of or delegation to bootstrap.sh's own `check_nizam_index`, since bootstrap.sh cannot be safely `source`d -- its last line unconditionally calls `main "$@"`, and sourcing it would re-run install/verify-only with the sourcing script's own argv and mutate the sourcing shell's options via its own `set -euo pipefail`) that parses `<target>/NIZAM.json` and confirms every path indexed under an injected module (`standard`/`templates`/`schema`/`tools`) resolves under `<target>` -- deliberately duplicating a SMALL, independent slice of bootstrap.sh's own logic so a latent bug in bootstrap.sh's self-check would not silently pass the harness too; this is a genuine second, independent proof, not a re-use of the thing under test. (c) `assert_discovery_path <target>` -- composes `tools/verify_lib.sh`'s `vlib_path_resolves "<target>/tools/skill.json"` directly (a concrete file path with no trailing `/`, so the directory exemption does not apply and the call genuinely tests existence), per spec's 'composes assertions from verify_lib.sh where applicable' -- this is the H4 regression guard (tools/interface.md Sec 2 item 1). (d) `run_bootstrap_verify_only <tag> <target>` -- `bash bootstrap.sh --verify-only --tag "${tag}" --target "${target}"`, asserting exit 0, proving bootstrap.sh's OWN self-check mechanism also functions end-to-end against the real injected target (a second, complementary proof to (b), not a substitute for it, since (b) protects against a latent bug in bootstrap.sh's own verifier).
design_notes.negative_self_test_semantics: Per spec's literal acceptance wording ('it MUST exit non-zero') `--self-test-negative`'s DESIGNED, documented exit contract is: this mode's job is to PROVE a failure detection, so completing that job (the guard correctly firing) is itself signalled by a non-zero exit -- mirroring the common negative-test-harness convention where 'the check under test correctly rejected bad input' is reported via the wrapper's own non-zero status, distinguished from an unexpected script crash only by the printed diagnostic. Concretely: after breaking the payload, `main` calls the SAME `assert_discovery_path` used by the positive path; if it (bug) still returns 0 against the broken target, `die` reports 'H4 REGRESSION: ... NOT load-bearing' (exit 1); if it (correct) returns non-zero, `main` logs 'H4 GUARD CONFIRMED LOAD-BEARING: ... tools/skill.json ... correctly rejected' and ALSO exits 1 (by explicit design, documented in the script's header comment) -- both branches are non-zero, satisfying the acceptance test literally, but the two are DISTINGUISHABLE by their printed message, and this contract's verification suite asserts the SPECIFIC 'CONFIRMED LOAD-BEARING' + 'tools/skill.json' text appears (never merely a generic non-zero), closing the vacuous-always-exit-1 gaming hole methodology/02 Sec 10 forbids.
design_notes.ndebt_004_decision: REVISED (rev1, per .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json rev0 issue with null verification_index): rev0's design_notes asserted 'no real R2 gap' and 'DEBT.md staying untouched constitutes consistency' WITHOUT ever quoting or reconciling against docs/planning/DEBT.md's own NDEBT-004 remediation text, which at rev0 time literally read 'Address in F-029 ... run `validate.sh --payload` against a real bootstrapped tree' -- the exact opposite of this contract's decision. The evaluator correctly flagged this as an unreconciled ledger contradiction (not a code defect). RESOLUTION: the orchestrator has since updated docs/planning/DEBT.md's NDEBT-004 remediation column (out-of-band, prior to this revision -- the generator does not and did not edit DEBT.md itself) to read: 'DEFERRED beyond F-029. F-029 (the hermetic e2e harness) is spec-scoped (product_spec_004 Sec 4.2) to bootstrap.sh's OWN --verify-only inject-to-verify assertions (payload present, index-valid, .nizam/tools/skill.json discovery, verify-only exit 0), NOT validate.sh --payload -- that would require editing C9 (a different check, out of F-029's scope; Mode A ruled the NO-decision spec-faithful). The validate.sh --payload consumer-safety diagnostic + any C9 payload skipped_dirs carve-out is deferred to a future self-compliance feature.' This contract's decision -- the harness does NOT additionally invoke `validate.sh --payload` against the bootstrapped `<target>` tree as a 5th assertion (the 'NO' branch) -- now MATCHES this reconciled ledger text VERBATIM; there is no remaining contradiction between this contract and NDEBT-004's own remediation column. Justification (unchanged from rev0, now cross-referenced against the reconciled ledger rather than asserted in isolation): (1) NONE of feature_list_004.json's 11 F-029 acceptance tests name `validate.sh` or `--payload` at all -- the one place `validate.sh` is named, AT11, explicitly requires this feature NOT to touch it. (2) product_spec_004.md Sec 4.2's own enumerated harness assertions are exactly (a) payload present, (b) index-valid, (c) discovery path, (d) `--verify-only` exits 0 -- `validate.sh --payload` is never named as a 5th assertion there; the spec text frames the harness as proving the 'real inject->verify adoption path' via bootstrap.sh's OWN verify mechanism, not validate.sh. (3) F-029's own feature_list description states outright: 'validate.sh count is unaffected (this is a CI job + harness, not a validate.sh check)'. (4) Adding an un-spec'd 5th assertion would risk EXACTLY the false-fail NDEBT-004 warns about (the scratch `<target>` genuinely lacks `methodology/` and `docs/architecture/`), forcing EITHER a same-feature C9 fix (editing tools/validate.sh, a C-check, explicitly a non-goal here and a scope-creep pattern every C9/C10/C11 feature has kept serialized into its OWN dedicated feature) OR a scope-narrowing workaround with no spec anchor -- neither serves R2's stated goal better than the four spec-named assertions already do, mirroring contract 027's declined 4th C10 sub-check and contract 028's declined `.agent/validator/` 4th artifact family. THEREFORE: NDEBT-004 REMAINS OPEN (unchanged date/severity; only its remediation text was updated by the orchestrator to the DEFERRED-beyond-F-029 disposition quoted above) -- this feature does not resolve, narrow, or newly reproduce it; the harness's --verify-only assertion exercises bootstrap.sh's OWN check_nizam_index/check_required_files/check_provenance_tag, never C9, so it generates no new evidence about C9's payload-mode behavior either way. docs/planning/DEBT.md is NOT edited by this contract's own implementation diff (non-goal; verification entry 20 confirms the generator's diff never touches it -- the orchestrator's own prior update to the file is a durable-state/ledger action outside this contract's scope, not something this feature's implementation performs or depends on performing).

files_create.purpose: Hermetic (network-free), dual-mode (sourceable library + directly-executable via a BASH_SOURCE-guarded main) harness. Sourcing defines exactly: `assert_payload_present`, `assert_nizam_index_valid`, `assert_discovery_path`, `run_bootstrap_verify_only`, `cleanup`, `main`, with zero side effects (mirrors tools/verify_lib.sh's F-023 sourced-library discipline). Direct execution (no args) performs one full ephemeral-tag + file:// clone + inject + assert cycle (payload-present, index-valid, discovery-path, --verify-only) against a mktemp scratch `.nizam` target, prints a final line matching `pass|ok|success` (case-insensitive) on success, and exits 0; `trap cleanup EXIT` unconditionally deletes the ephemeral annotated tag and the scratch dir even on failure, following bootstrap.sh's own `local exit_code=$?; ...; return "${exit_code}"` cleanup-trap idiom so cleanup never masks the real exit status. `--self-test-negative` runs an independent (separately tagged/scratched) install cycle, then deliberately removes `<target>/tools/skill.json`, re-invokes the SAME `assert_discovery_path` against the broken target, and exits non-zero either way (distinguished by message: 'H4 GUARD CONFIRMED LOAD-BEARING ... tools/skill.json ...' on genuine detection vs 'H4 REGRESSION ... NOT load-bearing' if the guard failed to fire) -- see design_notes.negative_self_test_semantics. Composes `tools/verify_lib.sh`'s `vlib_path_resolves` for the discovery-path assertion only (never for the directory-presence checks, which would be vacuously exempted by its trailing-slash directory-only rule). Does not source or modify bootstrap.sh; invokes it only as a subprocess (`bash bootstrap.sh --repo-url ... / --verify-only ...`). No `tools/fixtures/` files are needed or created -- the harness proves both its positive and negative paths against a REAL bootstrapped tree it creates itself each invocation, not static fixtures.

files_modify.purpose: Add a SECOND job, `e2e_bootstrap` (alongside the existing `validate` job, itself untouched), running `bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh`. Mirrors the `validate` job's action pins EXACTLY (no new/different SHAs): `actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2` + `persist-credentials: false`, and `actions/setup-python@0b93645e9fea7318ecaed2b359559ac225c90a2b # v5.3.0` (python3 is required by bootstrap.sh's own `check_nizam_index`/provenance-read steps and by this harness's independent `assert_nizam_index_valid`; NO `pip install jsonschema pyyaml` step is added -- unlike the `validate` job, neither bootstrap.sh nor this harness imports `jsonschema` or `yaml`, only python3's `json` stdlib module, so the extra pip step would be an unused dependency). REVISED (rev1, per .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json rev0 unsupported_claims finding): rev0 required `fetch-depth: 0` on this job's checkout step, justified by an UNSUPPORTED claim that a shallow source repository makes `git clone --depth 1` fail with `fatal: attempt to fetch/clone from a shallow repository`. The evaluator EMPIRICALLY REFUTED this (git 2.53.0): a real shallow clone (`--depth 1`, confirmed `git rev-parse --is-shallow-repository` = true, simulating CI's default fetch-depth:1) followed by creating an ephemeral annotated tag on ITS OWN HEAD and then `git clone --depth 1 --branch <tag> file://<that shallow checkout>` SUCCEEDED (exit 0, only a harmless 'is not a commit!' advisory about the annotated-tag object, no fatal error). The generator independently REPRODUCED this exact scenario in this revision (shallow clone -> tag on HEAD -> depth-1 file:// clone from the shallow source; exit 0, no fatal error) before deciding. DECISION: `fetch-depth: 0` is DROPPED from the new `e2e_bootstrap` job -- it is not needed, because the harness's ephemeral tag is created LOCALLY on the checkout's own HEAD (a commit object always present regardless of clone depth) and bootstrap.sh's `--depth 1` self-clone from that local checkout via `file://` succeeds regardless of whether the checkout itself is shallow or full, confirmed empirically rather than asserted. The `e2e_bootstrap` job's checkout step therefore uses the SAME pinned `actions/checkout` SHA as `validate` but WITHOUT a `fetch-depth` override (GitHub Actions' own default, effectively a shallow depth-1 checkout), keeping the new job's configuration minimal and not carrying an unfounded requirement. No change to the existing `validate` job (whose OWN `fetch-depth: 0` remains, for its own genuine, unrelated, and still-valid C8 git-show-HEAD reason), to `on:`/`permissions:`/`concurrency:` blocks, or to any other file.

non_goals: No modification to bootstrap.sh whatsoever (no local-path mode, no dry-run flag, no new CLI surface) -- spec Sec 4.2 confirms the existing --repo-url/--tag/--target/--verify-only surface is already sufficient; verified by AT4 (git diff --quiet HEAD -- bootstrap.sh)

No modification to tools/validate.sh -- this is a harness + CI job, not a validate.sh check; the SUMMARY count (currently 11 passed, 0 failed after F-028) is unaffected, per feature_list_004 F-029's own description and AT11

No invocation of `validate.sh --payload` (or any validate.sh mode) against the bootstrapped scratch target -- see design_notes.ndebt_004_decision: NDEBT-004 stays OPEN, remediation now recorded in docs/planning/DEBT.md as 'DEFERRED beyond F-029' (reconciled by the orchestrator to match this feature's spec-scoped (a)-(d) decision, per product_spec_004 Sec 4.2), unaddressed by this feature deliberately

No edit to docs/planning/DEBT.md by this feature's OWN implementation diff -- the orchestrator already reconciled NDEBT-004's remediation text to 'DEFERRED beyond F-029' out-of-band, prior to this revision; this contract's non-goal is that the generator's diff never touches the file, not that the file stays byte-identical to some earlier version
No modification to tools/verify_lib.sh (F-023 is complete and read-only for this feature) -- the harness composes vlib_path_resolves as-is, never editing the library
No new tools/fixtures/ files -- the harness's own --self-test-negative mode against a real bootstrapped tree supplies the negative-mode proof; no static fixture is needed

No edit to NIZAM.json, CHANGELOG.md, CONTEXT.md, README.md, schema/*.json, standard/, methodology/, registry/, templates/, or docs/ -- this feature's shipped-doc surface is exactly the harness script + the workflow file; no frontmatter/C1/C2/C3/C8 obligation is incurred since tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh is a `.sh` (not in the shipped-doc set, mirroring tools/validate.sh and tools/verify_lib.sh) and .github/workflows/compliance.yml is a `.yml` (also outside the shipped-doc set)
No bump to NIZAM.json framework.version and no touch to any framework-version anchor (C10's concern, already shipped)
No change to the existing `validate` job in .github/workflows/compliance.yml, nor to the workflow's `on:`/`permissions:`/`concurrency:` blocks -- strictly additive: one new job
No release, tag, or v0.6.0 gate action -- that is F-030's job

No edit to any immutable prior-phase/prior-feature artifact other than this feature's own .agent/contracts/029.json (once created) and the durable-state files the orchestrator (not the generator) updates
No push to any remote, no real network clone -- the harness's git clone is exclusively file:// against the local working checkout

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT1: harness exists and is syntactically valid: test -f tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh && bash -n tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh
verification.command: f=tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; bash -n "$f" && echo PASS || { echo "bash -n failed on $f"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT2: the harness runs hermetically and exits 0 on a clean checkout (file:// + ephemeral tag, no network), printing a pass/ok/success indication: out=$(bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh 2>&1); test $? -eq 0; echo "$out" | grep -Eqi 'pass|ok|success'
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "harness exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -Eqi 'pass|ok|success' || { echo "no pass/ok/success indication in output"; exit 1; }; echo VERIFY_PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT3: the harness uses a LOCAL file:// source and an ephemeral git tag, not a network clone of a published tag: grep -q 'file://' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh && grep -Eq 'git tag' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh
verification.command: f=tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh; grep -q 'file://' "$f" || { echo "no file:// usage found"; exit 1; }; grep -Eq 'git tag -a' "$f" || { echo "no annotated (git tag -a) usage found"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT4: bootstrap.sh is NOT modified by this feature: git diff --quiet HEAD -- bootstrap.sh
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- bootstrap.sh && echo PASS || { echo "bootstrap.sh was modified"; git diff HEAD -- bootstrap.sh; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT5: the harness asserts the documented .nizam/tools/skill.json discovery path (H4 regression guard): grep -q '.nizam/tools/skill.json' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh || grep -Eq 'tools/skill.json' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh
verification.command: grep -q 'tools/skill.json' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh && echo PASS || { echo "no tools/skill.json discovery-path assertion found"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT6: the harness asserts --verify-only passes against the injected target: grep -q -- '--verify-only' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh
verification.command: grep -q -- '--verify-only' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh && echo PASS || { echo "no --verify-only invocation found"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT7: the harness cleans up (ephemeral tag + scratch dir) via a trap even on failure: grep -Eq 'trap .*(cleanup|EXIT)' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh
verification.command: grep -Eq 'trap[[:space:]]+cleanup[[:space:]]+EXIT' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh && echo PASS || { echo "no 'trap cleanup EXIT' registration found"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT8: negative self-test: invoking the harness's broken-payload mode exits non-zero (the H4 guard is load-bearing): out=$(bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh --self-test-negative 2>&1 || echo NONZERO); echo "$out" | grep -q NONZERO
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh --self-test-negative 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected nonzero exit from --self-test-negative, got 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'CONFIRMED LOAD-BEARING' || { echo "missing the specific genuine-detection diagnostic -- possible vacuous always-fail stub"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'tools/skill.json' || { echo "diagnostic does not name the broken file"; exit 1; }; echo VERIFY_PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT9: a CI job runs the harness: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; w=yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/compliance.yml')); jobs=w.get('jobs',{}); ok=any('e2e_bootstrap_test.sh' in str(j) for j in jobs.values()); sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)"
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import yaml, sys
w = yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/compliance.yml', encoding='utf-8'))
jobs = w.get('jobs', {})
assert 'validate' in jobs, 'existing validate job missing -- regression'
ok = any('e2e_bootstrap_test.sh' in str(j) for name, j in jobs.items() if name != 'validate')
sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)
" && echo PASS || { echo "no CI job (other than the existing validate job) runs the harness"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT10 (HARDENED rev1, per .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json rev0 issue -- verification_index 10: the raw command false-passed vacuously when tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh does not exist, since the harness invocations' failures were swallowed by `|| true` and 'no leaked tag / no residue' is trivially true when nothing ran): the harness leaves no residue in the repo tree (ephemeral tag deleted, no scratch dir committed) -- but ONLY provably so if the harness actually exists AND its positive (default-mode) run actually succeeded; a missing file or a failed run must FAIL this check, not be silently swallowed into a vacuous PASS.
verification.command: f=tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f -- cannot prove no-residue for a harness that does not exist"; exit 1; }; out1=$(bash "$f" 2>&1); rc1=$?; echo "$out1"; test "$rc1" -eq 0 || { echo "harness (default/positive run) exited $rc1, expected 0 -- cannot prove genuine no-residue if the run under test did not actually succeed"; exit 1; }; bash "$f" --self-test-negative >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; test -z "$(git tag -l 'e2e-*')" || { echo "leaked e2e-* tag(s):"; git tag -l 'e2e-*'; exit 1; }; changed=$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- . ':(exclude).agent' | grep -Ev 'tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh|\.github/workflows/compliance\.yml'); test -z "$changed" || { echo "residue outside scope:"; echo "$changed"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-029 AT11: validate.sh count is UNCHANGED by this feature (harness + CI job, not a validate.sh check): bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: (8|9|10|11) passed, 0 failed'
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "validate.sh exited $rc"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: (8|9|10|11) passed, 0 failed' || { echo "unexpected SUMMARY line: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY:')"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (verification soundness, scope guard): only tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh and .github/workflows/compliance.yml changed outside .agent/ (untracked-aware guard, per methodology/02 Sec 10).
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh' '.github/workflows/compliance.yml' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (soundness, dual-mode/no-side-effect sourcing, F-023 precedent): sourcing the harness defines its assertion functions without executing main or any git/network operation.
verification.command: out=$(bash -c 'set -e; source tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh; for fn in assert_payload_present assert_nizam_index_valid assert_discovery_path run_bootstrap_verify_only cleanup main; do declare -F "$fn" >/dev/null || { echo "missing $fn"; exit 1; }; done; echo SOURCED_OK'); echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -qx SOURCED_OK && test -z "$(git tag -l 'e2e-*')" && echo PASS || { echo "sourcing had a side effect or a required function is missing"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity, directory-presence assertion, HARDENED rev1 per .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json rev0 issue -- verification_index 14: without `set -e`/explicit guards, a failed `source` of a nonexistent file plus an undefined `assert_payload_present` [rc 127, treated as boolean false by `if`] fell through to the SAME 'PASS' the real proof would print): assert_payload_present genuinely detects a MISSING injected module directory (not vacuously exempted by vlib_path_resolves' trailing-slash directory-only rule) -- fixture-tested in isolation via sourcing, without a full clone; the source and the function's existence are now explicitly gated BEFORE invocation.
verification.command: test -f tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh || { echo "MISSING tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh"; exit 1; }; source tools/verify_lib.sh; source tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh || { echo "failed to source tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh"; exit 1; }; declare -F assert_payload_present >/dev/null || { echo "assert_payload_present not defined after sourcing"; exit 1; }; scratch=$(mktemp -d); out=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -rf "$scratch"; rm -f "$out"' EXIT; mkdir -p "$scratch/standard" "$scratch/templates" "$scratch/schema"; echo '{}' > "$scratch/NIZAM.json"; echo '{}' > "$scratch/provenance.json"; if assert_payload_present "$scratch" >"$out" 2>&1; then echo "assert_payload_present incorrectly PASSED with tools/ missing"; cat "$out" 2>/dev/null; exit 1; fi; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity, index-validity assertion, HARDENED rev1 per .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json rev0 issue -- verification_index 15: identical false-pass mechanism as entry 14, reproduced verbatim): assert_nizam_index_valid genuinely detects an indexed path that fails to resolve under the target -- fixture-tested in isolation via sourcing; the source and the function's existence are now explicitly gated BEFORE invocation.
verification.command: test -f tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh || { echo "MISSING tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh"; exit 1; }; source tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh || { echo "failed to source tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh"; exit 1; }; declare -F assert_nizam_index_valid >/dev/null || { echo "assert_nizam_index_valid not defined after sourcing"; exit 1; }; scratch=$(mktemp -d); out=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -rf "$scratch"; rm -f "$out"' EXIT; mkdir -p "$scratch/standard"; python3 -c "import json; json.dump({'modules':[{'path':'standard','key_documents':['standard/DOES_NOT_EXIST.md']}]}, open('$scratch/NIZAM.json','w'))"; if assert_nizam_index_valid "$scratch" >"$out" 2>&1; then echo "assert_nizam_index_valid incorrectly PASSED against an unresolvable indexed path"; cat "$out" 2>/dev/null; exit 1; fi; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity, discovery-path assertion, HARDENED rev1 per .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json rev0 issue -- verification_index 16: identical false-pass mechanism as entries 14/15, reproduced verbatim): assert_discovery_path genuinely detects a missing tools/skill.json -- fixture-tested in isolation via sourcing (the same underlying property the full --self-test-negative proves end-to-end, re-proven here at the unit level); the source and the function's existence are now explicitly gated BEFORE invocation.
verification.command: test -f tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh || { echo "MISSING tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh"; exit 1; }; source tools/verify_lib.sh; source tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh || { echo "failed to source tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh"; exit 1; }; declare -F assert_discovery_path >/dev/null || { echo "assert_discovery_path not defined after sourcing"; exit 1; }; scratch=$(mktemp -d); out=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -rf "$scratch"; rm -f "$out"' EXIT; mkdir -p "$scratch/tools"; if assert_discovery_path "$scratch" >"$out" 2>&1; then echo "assert_discovery_path incorrectly PASSED with tools/skill.json missing"; cat "$out" 2>/dev/null; exit 1; fi; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (idempotency/no cross-run residue accumulation): two consecutive full harness invocations both succeed and neither leaves a leaked tag behind, proving the mechanism is safely repeatable (e.g. under CI retries) rather than a one-shot script.
verification.command: run1=$(mktemp); run2=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -f "$run1" "$run2"' EXIT; bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >"$run1" 2>&1; rc1=$?; bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >"$run2" 2>&1; rc2=$?; cat "$run1"; cat "$run2"; test "$rc1" -eq 0 && test "$rc2" -eq 0 || { echo "one of two consecutive runs failed (rc1=$rc1 rc2=$rc2)"; exit 1; }; test -z "$(git tag -l 'e2e-*')" || { echo "leaked tag(s) after two runs:"; git tag -l 'e2e-*'; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (CI hardening parity, REVISED rev1 per .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json rev0 unsupported_claims finding -- the fetch-depth:0 requirement was dropped, see design_notes/scope for the empirically-reproduced refutation): the new e2e_bootstrap job pins the SAME action SHAs as the existing validate job (no divergent/unpinned action versions); no fetch-depth assertion is made, since fetch-depth:0 is deliberately NOT required on this job.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import yaml, sys
w = yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/compliance.yml', encoding='utf-8'))
jobs = w.get('jobs', {})
assert 'validate' in jobs
e2e_name = next((n for n in jobs if n != 'validate'), None)
assert e2e_name, 'no second job found'
e2e = jobs[e2e_name]
steps = e2e.get('steps', [])
uses = [s.get('uses','') for s in steps]
assert any('actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683' in u for u in uses), 'checkout SHA pin mismatch or missing'
assert any('actions/setup-python@0b93645e9fea7318ecaed2b359559ac225c90a2b' in u for u in uses), 'setup-python SHA pin mismatch or missing'
print('CONFIRMED: e2e job', e2e_name, 'action pins match the validate job')
" && echo PASS || { echo "CI job SHA-pin parity check failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, HARDENED rev1 per .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json rev0 issue -- verification_index 19: the raw `! grep -q ... && echo PASS` is the 'Vacuous Negated Grep' anti-pattern methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md Sec 3.1 names by that title -- it false-passed when tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh does not exist, since grep's nonzero exit for a missing FILE is indistinguishable from its nonzero exit for a missing PATTERN): the harness never invokes validate.sh in any mode (NDEBT-004 decision -- see design_notes.ndebt_004_decision; this feature deliberately does not add a 5th validate.sh --payload assertion). The negated grep is now preceded by an explicit existence/non-empty check per methodology/02 Sec 3.1's own prescribed remedy.
verification.command: test -s tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh || { echo "MISSING or empty tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh"; exit 1; }; ! grep -q 'validate.sh' tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh && echo PASS || { echo "tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh unexpectedly references validate.sh -- out of scope per this contract's NDEBT-004 decision"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): docs/planning/DEBT.md is untouched by this feature (NDEBT-004 stays open, unchanged).
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/DEBT.md && echo PASS || { echo "docs/planning/DEBT.md was modified -- out of scope for this contract"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/029-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/029-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/029-verify-20.txt for the twentieth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 20 entries; CORRECTED rev1 per .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json rev0 observation, which caught this off-by-one against the actual, directly-counted array length). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
